5.Migration
5.1mdbook Guide5.2MkDocs Guide5.3Obsidian Guide

Type to search titles, headings, and page content.

Search is ready. Try a page title, a section heading, or a phrase from the content.

Migration

Use marque migrate when you already have a markdown-first docs project and want a clean Marque site without rebuilding everything by hand.

The first migration pass is intentionally narrow: Marque currently targets the tools that are structurally closest to it.

Supported first

mdBook

book.toml, src/SUMMARY.md, and src/**/*.md map very naturally onto marque.toml, summary.mq, and pages/**/*.mq.

MkDocs

mkdocs.yml plus docs/**/*.md also translates well, especially when the site uses a normal nav: tree and markdown pages.

Not first-pass

Tools with heavy framework runtime assumptions, MDX, JSX, plugin APIs, or template languages need a looser migration strategy and are not the first targets.

Command

Auto-detect

sh
marque migrate ./old-docs

This creates a new site next to the source project at ./old-docs-marque.

Explicit source

sh
marque migrate ./old-book ./new-marque --from mdbook
marque migrate ./old-site ./new-marque --from mkdocs

Choose layout and theme

sh
marque migrate ./docs ./docs-marque --from mkdocs --layout sidebar --theme comte

What gets migrated

Content

Markdown pages are copied into pages/ and renamed from .md to .mq.

Navigation

src/SUMMARY.md from mdBook or nav: from MkDocs becomes summary.mq.

Assets

Non-markdown files from the source docs tree are copied into static/ with matching relative paths.

Internal links

Local markdown links are rewritten from .md targets to the matching .mq pages so Marque can resolve them to HTML.

Site config

The migrated site gets a fresh marque.toml using the imported site title and description when they are available.

Review page

Marque also creates pages/migration-notes.mq so any skipped metadata or unsupported features are visible inside the new site.

What still needs a human pass

Review the generated summary.mq and confirm labels, nesting, and ordering.

Rebuild the original visual design as a Marque theme or layout instead of expecting source themes to carry over automatically.

Check plugins, preprocessors, extra JavaScript, MDX, or framework-specific markup by hand.

Open the generated migration-notes.mq page and clear every warning before treating the migration as finished.

Structure mapping

mdBook

txt
book.toml        -> marque.toml
src/SUMMARY.md   -> summary.mq
src/**/*.md      -> pages/**/*.mq
src/** assets    -> static/**

MkDocs

txt
mkdocs.yml       -> marque.toml + summary.mq
docs/**/*.md     -> pages/**/*.mq
docs/** assets   -> static/**

Migration is meant to get you most of the way there quickly, not to promise a pixel-perfect or plugin-perfect import.

Built with Marque
